*— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-04-09T23:07:25Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29439#issuecomment-4218348025","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws77cTVj","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"PR: #31857

After reviewing the discussion, literature, and RHEA reactions, here is what I did:

**GO:0008253 (5'-nucleotidase activity)** -- definition broadened from ribo-specific to general. Both PMID:27907199 and PMID:34568961 confirm that EC:3.1.3.5 enzymes "catalyze the hydrolytic dephosphorylation of 5'-ribonucleotides and 5'-deoxyribonucleotides." The new definition uses the RHEA:22140 reaction equation: "a nucleoside 5'-phosphate + H2O = a nucleoside + phosphate." The name "5'-nucleotidase activity" is retained as-is, per @raymond91125's recommendation.

**GO:0002953 (5'-deoxynucleotidase activity)** -- reparented from GO:0016791 (phosphatase activity) to GO:0008253 (5'-nucleotidase activity). Since the broader 5'-nucleotidase now covers both ribo and deoxy, 5'-deoxynucleotidase (EC:3.1.3.89) is logically a subtype.

**GO:0008252 (nucleotidase activity)** -- removed the RHEA:22140 xref, which was incorrectly mapped as exactMatch. RHEA:22140 is 5'-position-specific, while GO:0008252 is position-nonspecific. The RHEA:22140 mapping now lives on GO:0008253 where it belongs. Note: the def xref still cites RHEA:22140 as provenance, which could be revisited in a separate ticket if desired.

**RHEA xref corrections on GO:0008253**: RHEA:12484 (ribo-specific) demoted from exactMatch to narrowMatch. RHEA:22140 (general) added as exactMatch.

All validation passes (SPARQL QC + ELK reasoning).

### Research

## PMID:27907199 "Identification, Heterologous Expression, and Functional Characterization of Bacillus subtilis YutF, a HAD Superfamily 5'-Nucleotidase with Broad Substrate Specificity."

Zakataeva NP et al. PLoS One (2016).

* SUPPORT: PMID:27907199 "5'-nucleotidases (EC 3.1.3.5) catalyze the hydrolytic dephosphorylation of 5'-ribonucleotides and 5'-deoxyribonucleotides as well as complex nucleotides, such as uridine 5'-diphosphoglucose (UDP-glucose), nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide and flavin adenine dinucleotide, to their corresponding nucleosides plus phosphate."

## PMID:34568961 "Microbial 5'-nucleotidases: their characteristics, roles in cellular metabolism, and possible practical applications."

Zakataeva NP. Appl Microbiol Biotechnol (2021).

* SUPPORT: PMID:34568961 "5'-Nucleotidases (EC 3.1.3.5) are enzymes that catalyze the hydrolytic dephosphorylation of 5'-ribonucleotides and 5'-deoxyribonucleotides to their respective nucleosides and phosphate."

*— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-04-10T23:18:46Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29439#issuecomment-4227291366","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7799Pv","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"## Research: EC and RHEA status for GO:0008252 nucleotidase activity

### EC:3.1.3.31 — deleted, no successor

EC:3.1.3.31 ("nucleotidase") was created in 1972 and **deleted in 2020** by the NC-IUBMB. It was not transferred to any other EC number — deleted outright. The IUBMB comment on the original entry noted it had "a wide specificity for 2′-, 3′- and 5′-nucleotides" and could also hydrolyse glycerol phosphate and 4-nitrophenyl phosphate, with a footnote that "the activity may be that of an acid phosphatase" (EC:3.1.3.2). That footnote was effectively the justification for the deletion: the activity was never shown to be a distinct enzyme from non-specific acid phosphatase.

**Consequence for GO:0008252**: there is no direct EC replacement. The only EC numbers in this area are position-specific children:

| EC | Enzyme | GO term |
|---|---|---|
| 3.1.3.5 | 5'-nucleotidase | GO:0008253 |
| 3.1.3.6 | 3'-nucleotidase | GO:0008254 |
| 3.1.3.89 | 5'-deoxynucleotidase | GO:0002953 |
| 3.1.3.99 | IMP-specific 5'-nucleotidase | GO:0050483 (xref missing) |

GO:0008252 sits above all of these as a position-agnostic grouping class, but no single EC number corresponds to it anymore.

### RHEAs — no exact match, only position-specific narrow matches

There is no position-nonspecific RHEA reaction for nucleotidase activity. The two relevant RHEAs are both position-specific:

- **RHEA:22140** — `a nucleoside 5'-phosphate + H2O = a nucleoside + phosphate` — corresponds to EC:3.1.3.5 (already exactMatch on GO:0008253 in PR #31857)
- **RHEA:10144** — `a ribonucleoside 3'-phosphate + H2O = a ribonucleoside + phosphate` — corresponds to EC:3.1.3.6 (already exactMatch on GO:0008254)

The current GO:0008252 def text `"a nucleotide + H2O = a nucleoside + phosphate"` is position-neutral and does not faithfully correspond to any single RHEA reaction. The existing `[RHEA:22140]` provenance in the def is inconsistent, since RHEA:22140 is 5'-specific and has already been correctly reassigned to GO:0008253 in #31857. On GO:0008252, the best we can do is narrowMatch xrefs to both RHEA:22140 and RHEA:10144, reflecting that the parent covers both child reactions.

### Proper reference for the definition

Since the EC citation is gone, I recommend replacing `[RHEA:22140]` in the def provenance with a general nucleotidase review. Verified PMIDs:

- **PMID:12947102** — Bianchi V, Spychala J. "Mammalian 5'-nucleotidases." *J Biol Chem* 278(47):46195-8 (2003). Classifies nucleotidases as a group.
- **PMID:22555564** — Zimmermann H, Zebisch M, Sträter N. "Cellular function and molecular structure of ecto-nucleotidases." *Purinergic Signal* 8(3):437-502 (2012). Comprehensive review covering the nucleotidase families and their catalytic mechanisms.

### Proposed follow-up for PR #31857

If you agree, I can extend PR #31857 with:

1. Replace `[RHEA:22140]` in GO:0008252 def with `[PMID:12947102, PMID:22555564]`
2. Add `xref: RHEA:22140 {source=\"skos:narrowMatch\"}` and `xref: RHEA:10144 {source=\"skos:narrowMatch\"}` to GO:0008252 (it currently has no xrefs at all after the RHEA:22140 exactMatch is removed)

No EC xref gets added to GO:0008252 — the term stays EC-free, reflecting that its namesake EC:3.1.3.31 is gone.

Let me know if you want me to make these edits, or if you'd prefer a different treatment (e.g., keep GO:0008252 without any RHEA xrefs to avoid the grouping-class-with-narrow-matches pattern).

### EC:3.1.3.31 — deleted, no successor

EC:3.1.3.31 ("nucleotidase") was created in 1972 and **deleted in 2020** by the NC-IUBMB. It was not transferred to any other EC number — deleted outright. The IUBMB comment on the original entry noted it had "a wide specificity for 2′-, 3′- and 5′-nucleotides" and could also hydrolyse glycerol phosphate and 4-nitrophenyl phosphate, with a footnote that "the activity may be that of an acid phosphatase" (EC:3.1.3.2). That footnote was effectively the justification for the deletion: the activity was never shown to be a distinct enzyme from non-specific acid phosphatase.

**Consequence for GO:0008252**: there is no direct EC replacement. The only EC numbers in this area are position-specific children:

| EC | Enzyme | GO term |
|---|---|---|
| 3.1.3.5 | 5'-nucleotidase | GO:0008253 |
| 3.1.3.6 | 3'-nucleotidase | GO:0008254 |
| 3.1.3.89 | 5'-deoxynucleotidase | GO:0002953 |
| 3.1.3.99 | IMP-specific 5'-nucleotidase | GO:0050483 (xref missing) |

GO:0008252 sits above all of these as a position-agnostic grouping class, but no single EC number corresponds to it anymore.

### RHEAs — no exact match, only position-specific narrow matches

There is no position-nonspecific RHEA reaction for nucleotidase activity. The two relevant RHEAs are both position-specific:

- **RHEA:22140** — `a nucleoside 5'-phosphate + H2O = a nucleoside + phosphate` — corresponds to EC:3.1.3.5 (already exactMatch on GO:0008253 in PR #31857)
- **RHEA:10144** — `a ribonucleoside 3'-phosphate + H2O = a ribonucleoside + phosphate` — corresponds to EC:3.1.3.6 (already exactMatch on GO:0008254)

The current GO:0008252 def text `"a nucleotide + H2O = a nucleoside + phosphate"` is position-neutral and does not faithfully correspond to any single RHEA reaction. The existing `[RHEA:22140]` provenance in the def is inconsistent, since RHEA:22140 is 5'-specific and has already been correctly reassigned to GO:0008253 in #31857. On GO:0008252, the best we can do is narrowMatch xrefs to both RHEA:22140 and RHEA:10144, reflecting that the parent covers both child reactions.

### Proper reference for the definition

Since the EC citation is gone, I recommend replacing `[RHEA:22140]` in the def provenance with a general nucleotidase review. Verified PMIDs:

- **PMID:12947102** — Bianchi V, Spychala J. "Mammalian 5'-nucleotidases." *J Biol Chem* 278(47):46195-8 (2003). Classifies nucleotidases as a group.
- **PMID:22555564** — Zimmermann H, Zebisch M, Sträter N. "Cellular function and molecular structure of ecto-nucleotidases." *Purinergic Signal* 8(3):437-502 (2012). Comprehensive review covering the nucleotidase families and their catalytic mechanisms.

### Proposed follow-up for PR #31857

If you agree, I can extend PR #31857 with:

1. Replace `[RHEA:22140]` in GO:0008252 def with `[PMID:12947102, PMID:22555564]`
2. Add `xref: RHEA:22140 {source=\"skos:narrowMatch\"}` and `xref: RHEA:10144 {source=\"skos:narrowMatch\"}` to GO:0008252 (it currently has no xrefs at all after the RHEA:22140 exactMatch is removed)

# Paper Information

**Title:** "Cellular function and molecular structure of ecto-nucleotidases"

**Authors:** Herbert Zimmermann, Matthias Zebisch, and Norbert Sträter

**Journal:** Purinergic Signal

**Year:** 2012 (September; Volume 8, Issue 3, pages 437-502)

**About Ecto-nucleotidases:** Yes, this is entirely focused on ecto-nucleotidases. The abstract states that these enzymes "play a pivotal role in purinergic signal transmission" by hydrolyzing extracellular nucleotides. The review examines four major enzyme groups and discusses their biochemical properties, structural characteristics, and physiological functions.

GO:0008252: replace RHEA:22140 def xref with PMID references (issue #29439)

EC:3.1.3.31 (the original namesake of GO:0008252) was deleted by NC-IUBMB
in 2020 without a successor, having been deemed indistinct from non-specific
acid phosphatase. There is no single RHEA reaction that corresponds to the
position-nonspecific "nucleotidase activity" — the available RHEAs are
position-specific (RHEA:22140 = 5'-nucleotide hydrolysis = GO:0008253;
RHEA:10144 = 3'-nucleotide hydrolysis = GO:0008254). The previous
[RHEA:22140] def provenance on GO:0008252 was therefore inappropriate, and
the corresponding skos:exactMatch xref has already been removed in this PR.

Definition provenance now cites two general nucleotidase reviews:
- PMID:12947102 Bianchi V, Spychala J. "Mammalian 5'-nucleotidases."
  J Biol Chem 278(47):46195-8 (2003).
- PMID:22555564 Zimmermann H, Zebisch M, Sträter N. "Cellular function
  and molecular structure of ecto-nucleotidases." Purinergic Signal
  8(3):437-502 (2012).

Note: narrowMatch xrefs to RHEA:22140 and RHEA:10144 were considered for
GO:0008252 but rejected — the one-to-one-xrefs-by-value SPARQL constraint
forbids the same RHEA appearing on multiple GO terms with exact/narrow
scope, and these RHEAs are already exactMatch on the position-specific
children.

term_tracker_item for #29439 added alongside existing #30193.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

### Background

EC:3.1.3.31 (the original namesake of GO:0008252) was **deleted by NC-IUBMB in 2020** without a transfer or successor. The IUBMB footnote on the original entry said "the activity may be that of an acid phosphatase" (EC:3.1.3.2) — i.e. it was never shown to be a distinct enzyme. So GO:0008252 has no EC anchor.

For RHEA, there is no position-nonspecific reaction. The two relevant RHEAs are both position-specific and already exactMatched on the children:

|---|---|---|

| RHEA:10144 | 3'-phosphate hydrolysis | GO:0008254 (3'-nucleotidase) |
